description: A plain candid operational photograph (one of a paired set in the tweet) with no added graphic design or on-image text. It shows four law-enforcement officers walking away from the camera down an institutional corridor inside a jail. The officers wear dark jackets with large yellow back lettering reading 'POLICE' over agency identifiers: two read 'HSI' (Homeland Security Investigations) and one reads 'ERO' (Enforcement and Removal Operations); a detainee in a dark jacket and jeans is being escorted in the middle of the group. A colorful painted wall mural of a city skyline is visible, with text reading 'COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES' and what appears to be an inmate reception center. The setting is a controlled jail-transfer environment. The tweet uses this image to contrast a 'safe, controlled' jail handover against an at-large street arrest. This is documentary photography, not a designed meme/poster.
notable_text: POLICE HSI | POLICE ERO | COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (painted wall mural)
